title: The Written and Oral Dimension of Culinary Recipes
author: Panoschi, Silvia-Anamaria
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Abstract: As a text type, the culinary recipe belongs to the category of procedural texts, and as a communicative activity, its purpose is to present the process of preparing a dish. This type of text exhibits different formal characteristics depending on whether it is in written or oral form. In this article, we conduct a comparative analysis of a corpus of written recipes by M. Kogălniceanu and Costache Negruzzi, Mihai Lupescu, and others, alongside a corpus of oral culinary recipes collected through field surveys as part of the eCULTFOOD project. By analyzing these texts, we aim to highlight defining features of the culinary recipe's profile.
steps_raw: Abstract: As a text type, the culinary recipe belongs to the category of procedural texts, and as a communicative activity, its purpose is to present the process of preparing a dish. By analyzing these texts, we aim to highlight defining features of the culinary recipe's profile.
keywords: culinary recipe; oral; written; procedure; expressiveness
